content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of N integers.
For each element in the array, find the next greater element to its right.

The Next Greater Element (NGE) for an element x is the first element to the right of x that is strictly greater than x.

If no such element exists, output -1 for that position.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N space‑separated integers. The i‑th integer is the Next Greater Element to the right of the i‑th array element, or -1 if none exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For 4 the first greater on the right is 5; for 5 it is 25; for 2 it is 25; for 25 there is none, so -1.
Each element's next greater is the immediate next element, except the last which has none.
The array is strictly decreasing, so no element has a greater one to its right.

solution.approach: Traverse the array from right to left while maintaining a stack that stores candidate greater elements in strictly decreasing order. For each element arr[i], pop elements from the stack that are less than or equal to arr[i]; the element now on top (if any) is the next greater element. Record it, then push arr[i] onto the stack. This yields O(N) time per test case and O(N) auxiliary space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the stack and the output array.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        nge = [-1] * n
        stack = []  # monotonic decreasing stack of values
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            while stack and stack[-1] <= arr[i]:
                stack.pop()
            nge[i] = stack[-1] if stack else -1
            stack.append(arr[i])
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, nge)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
